discovery_instructions: Identify what the main item/entity is for this feature (e.g., employee record, document, invitation)
Look for list/table views - the rows represent the primary entity
Check page headers and breadcrumbs for entity naming
Note the singular and plural forms used in the UI

find_trigger: Look for buttons with: + Add, + Create, + New, plus icon
Check top-right corner of the page
Look in empty state messages for create prompts
create.validation: New item appears in list with unique identifier

find_trigger: Click on any row in a list/table
Look for 'View' or eye icon buttons
Check if rows are clickable (cursor changes)
read.validation: Detail panel, modal, or page opens showing full information

find_trigger: Look for Edit button, pencil icon
Check for inline editing (click on field to edit)
Look for status change dropdowns or buttons
Check action menus (three-dot menu) for edit option
update.validation: Changes persist after save, reflected in list view

find_trigger: Look for Delete button, trash icon
Check action menus (three-dot menu) for delete option
Look for bulk delete with checkboxes
Some features use 'Archive' or 'Cancel' instead of delete
delete.validation: Item removed from list or status changed to deleted/archived

instructions: Navigate to the feature's main section using the navigation_map
Take a full page snapshot to understand the layout
Find ALL clickable elements: buttons, links, tabs, dropdowns, icons
Map the navigation structure: tabs, sidebar items, breadcrumbs
Click each tab/sub-section and document what appears
Locate CRUD controls for the primary entity
Check for action menus (three-dot menus, right-click menus)
Document any modals, dialogs, slide-out panels
Identify form fields and input types when forms appear
Look for filters, search, sort, and export options
Check for bulk action controls (checkboxes, select all)
Note any settings or configuration options
discovery_targets: List/table of items with row actions

content_clusters.consolidation_notes: Merged three articles covering employee additions (new employees, already-insured members, and tracking) with seven PRDs covering AI automation, admin configuration, conditional documents, and regional expansion. Articles provide user-facing workflows; PRDs add technical implementation details, configuration requirements, and conditional logic.

Consolidated articles on adding and removing dependents with PRDs on React migration and conditional documents. Both articles follow similar workflows (navigate, upload docs, submit); PRDs add step restructuring and conditional logic for dependent-specific requirements.

product_context.title: PRD Endorsement AI Automation V1
product_context.why_built: Endorsements (employee additions and removals from insurance) are the most common administrative tasks for health insurance clients, processing approximately 2600 monthly. Manual processing is time-consuming and error-prone. AI automation can verify endorsement documents and extract data automatically, reducing processing time and improving accuracy. Target: 85% of endorsements processed with zero human intervention and 95% quality rate.
key_design_decisions: AI verification triggered automatically on document upload with progress indicator
If AI verification fails, user has three options: Re-upload, Verify manually (with pre-filled data), or Ignore and continue (with warning)
AI fallback: If Claude unreachable or timeout, system defaults to manual verification
Document order standardized: Passport, Visa, Emirates ID, Others
Sponsor matching required for employee additions only (not dependents) - compares establishment card with visa sponsor
Passport MRZ extraction preferred for standardization (handles known Syrian passport issues)
Feature flag controls gradual rollout to specific companies then global

product_context.title: PRD - Phase 1 - Expanding endorsements to other insurers
product_context.why_built: Enable flexible admin panel configuration for endorsement requirements per insurer. Phase 1 moves Top 5 insurers' configuration from hardcoded to configurable setup. Internal team can configure Addition Details (fields to show), Addition Documents (required/conditional documents per policy type), Removals (card number, visa cancellation, document sections), and Templates (downloadable document templates with helper text).
key_design_decisions: Admin panel configuration per insurance provider and policy type
Configurable fields for Addition Details with Show Field=Yes/No toggle
Predefined document sections (Passport, Photo, Visa, Change Status, Emirates ID) can be set as required/conditional
Custom document sections can be added beyond predefined ones
Removal configuration includes card number and visa cancellation inputs
Document templates with helper text and {Member name} placeholder support
Endorsements menu visibility based on company's relevant policies

product_context.title: PRD - Phase 2 - Expanding endorsements to other insurers
product_context.why_built: Expand endorsement configuration capabilities beyond Top 5 insurers to include remaining insurers (DNIRC, Generali, Morgan Price, Al Ain Ahlia-Expacare, Oriental Insurance). Create common census formats for additions and removals, add new document types and configurable fields, set TAT configurations, and enable payment tracking for endorsements across all insurers.
key_design_decisions: Common census formats for addition and removal per insurer
New document types: Certificate of Continuity, Undertaking Letter for Infants, Health Declaration Form, Basma Form
Configurable detail fields: COC Date, Visa Issue Date, Date of Marriage, Date Entered Country, Date of Hire, Sponsored By
TAT (Turn Around Time) configuration per policy type for additions and removals
Census template download in correct format for each insurer
Payment tracking for endorsements with 'Awaiting Client Payment' status
Date field validation: COC Date, Visa Issue Date, Date of Marriage, Date Entered Country must be today or past; Date of Hire within 30 days future or past

product_context.title: Endorsement - Addition Flow React Migration - PRD
product_context.why_built: Migrate endorsement addition flow to React with improved UX by restructuring from 2 steps to 3 steps: (1) Required Documents, (2) Member Details, (3) Additional Documents. Introduce document status tracking ("Not Complete"/"Complete") and collapsible document cards to help users easily capture uploaded vs remaining documents. Goal is to organize the flow better and help users detect all required documents in advance.
key_design_decisions: Three-step flow: Step 1 (Required Documents), Step 2 (Member Details), Step 3 (Additional Documents)
Document status tracking: "Not Complete" (default) → "Complete" (when uploaded) → reverts to "Not Complete" if removed
Collapsible document cards (collapsed by default) for Required Documents in Step 1
Auto-population of personal details and work details from employee profile in Step 2
OCR integration for document details extraction (Emirates ID, Passport No, UID, Resident file number) in Step 1
Conditional and optional documents appear in Step 3
Step navigation: Users must complete each step before proceeding

product_context.title: PRD Endorsement Conditional Documents
product_context.why_built: Reduce endorsement errors and back-and-forth communication by implementing conditional document requirements that dynamically become mandatory based on employee conditions (age, pregnancy, gender). Address main MAF (Medical Application Form) error causes: wrong template usage and documents becoming required due to specific conditions. Goals: decrease client delays in completing endorsements, reduce time spent by endorsement team giving feedback, and improve TAT (turnaround time).
key_design_decisions: Conditional documents configured in admin panel with yes/no questions
Questions assigned to Female or Male employees (gender-based visibility)
If user answers "Yes" to any question, document becomes mandatory
If user answers "No" to all questions, document upload component hides (not required)
Conditional guidance text displayed with questions
MAF template versioning with release date notifications
Conditional documents only available for "Not Required" documents (cannot add conditions to already-required documents)

product_context.title: AUD Endorsement Opportunity Assessment
product_context.why_built: Enable Abu Dhabi-based clients to submit insurance endorsement requests (additions/deletions) through the platform instead of via email. Guide users through required documents based on specific parameters to reduce back-and-forth communication with endorsement team, decrease turnaround time (TAT), and prevent late addition penalties. Support company strategic objective to expand into Abu Dhabi market and acquire more clients where investor focus lies.
key_design_decisions: Online endorsement submission replaces email-based process (endorsement@bayzat.com)
System guides users on specific documents required based on endorsement parameters
Document completeness validation before submission
Fast processing to avoid late addition penalties
Support for Abu Dhabi market expansion strategy

product_context.title: PRD - AUD Online Endorsement
product_context.why_built: Enable online endorsement requests for Abu Dhabi insurance policies, replacing the current email-based process that causes delays and back-and-forth communication. Allow endorsement team to configure different document requirements per emirate (Abu Dhabi vs Dubai, RAK, Al Fujeirah, Sharjah) and per insurance provider. Goals include: reducing turnaround time for adding employees to insurance plans, providing customers clear upfront document requirements, and supporting company objective to acquire more clients from Abu Dhabi market (key investor focus).
key_design_decisions: Multiple endorsement configurations per provider (one per emirate)
Default configuration for existing UAE settings (Dubai, RAK, Al Fujeirah, Sharjah)
New configurations disabled by default when created
Emirate-specific document requirements and templates
Admin panel central hub for endorsement configuration management
Audit tracking (creator, update date) for all configurations

detected_integrations.description: Source of employee and dependent information. Auto-populates personal details, work details, and health card information in endorsement forms. Dependent profiles linked to insurance management.
Core system managing insurance policies, plan assignments, and policy rosters. Endorsements add/remove employees and dependents from active plans.

Handles document submission, storage, and retrieval. Supports Passport, Visa, Emirates ID, health cards, marriage/birth certificates, and custom documents. AI verification extracts data from uploaded documents.
Sends notifications for endorsement status changes, 'Attention Required' alerts, and missing document requests. Replaces email-based endorsement submission process.
Centralized management of all endorsement requests (additions, removals, dependent changes). Tracks status through stages: Under Review, With Insurer, Attention Required, Completed.
Allows HR admins to configure endorsement requirements per insurance provider and policy type. Controls required documents, detail fields, removal options, templates, and TAT settings.

Automates document verification and data extraction for Passport, Visa, and Emirates ID. Validates documents against rules and extracts information for auto-population. Fallback to manual verification if AI unreachable.
Used for sponsor matching on employee additions. AI verification compares visa sponsor with establishment card to validate employee eligibility.
Integrates employee removal with departure process. Offboarding option available when removal reason is not 'Transferring to New Policy'. Offboarded employees lose Bayzat access.
External insurance provider systems receive endorsement requests and process additions/removals. Status updates flow back to Bayzat platform. Supports multiple insurers with different requirements.
Tracks endorsement status including 'Awaiting Client Payment' state. Supports payment tracking for endorsements across all insurers.
Generates and provides downloadable COI after endorsement completion. Available for 30-day window after completion.

check_instructions: Navigate to Automations in the left sidebar
Click + Create workflow (or similar create button)
In trigger selection, search for this feature's name
Check if this feature appears as a trigger source
Document any trigger events found (e.g., 'item created', 'status changed')
Check Actions section for actions related to this feature
Look for 'Approval' or 'Pending Approval' status in the feature
Check if items have approval workflow indicators
Look for 'Submit for Approval' buttons or actions
Find approval settings in feature configuration
Document the approval chain (who approves what)
Test approval/rejection flow if test data available
Check for approval history or audit trail

what_to_do.task: Submit new employee addition to health insurance
steps: Navigate to Endorsements tab or Employee Profile
Click 'New Addition' or 'Add to Insurance'
Select employee to add
Choose submission method (from Endorsement Tab or Employee Profile)
Upload required documents (Passport, Visa, Emirates ID per insurer configuration)
Fill in member details (personal, document, work information)
Add optional or conditional documents if applicable
Review extracted data from AI verification (if enabled)
Save as draft or submit endorsement request
Track endorsement status in Endorsements section
what_to_do.expected_outcome: Employee addition request submitted with all required documents and details. Request enters processing workflow and can be tracked online. AI verification (if enabled) extracts document data automatically, reducing manual entry errors.
rules_and_constraints: Endorsement can be submitted from two locations: Endorsement Tab or Employee Profile
All required documents per insurer/plan configuration must be uploaded before submission
Some documents may be conditional (required based on employee conditions like age, gender, pregnancy) or optional
Member details must include personal information, document details, and work information
Effective dates may change during processing (indicated by info icon)
For employee additions: Sponsor must match establishment card (AI verification validates this)
Document order standardized: 1. Passport 2. Visa 3. Emirates ID 4. Others
If AI verification fails, user can re-upload, verify manually, or ignore and continue with warning
Must upload specific documents based on dependent type (health card, marriage/birth certificate)
Must have valid relationship documentation (marriage/birth certificates)
Must have employee's health card if dependent is sponsored by employee
If documents were previously uploaded during employee profile creation, no need to re-upload
Users can save drafts of endorsement requests to complete later
Must complete submission by clicking 'Continue Submission' to finalize
Conditional documents may be required based on dependent age, gender, or other conditions
When removing an employee with dependents, all documents for both employee and dependents must be uploaded
Dependents cannot remain on a policy after the principal member is removed
Offboarding option available when removal reason is not 'Transferring to New Policy'
Offboarded employees lose Bayzat access after departure date
Removal reason must be specified
Can save drafts of endorsement requests to complete later
Users must upload all documents as specified in the feedback before submission
AI verification (if enabled) automatically extracts document data and validates against rules
For employee additions: Sponsor must match establishment card (AI validates this)
If AI is unreachable, system defaults to manual verification
edge_cases: Users can save draft endorsement requests and complete later
If documents were previously uploaded during employee profile creation, no need to re-upload
AI verification may timeout or be unreachable - system defaults to manual verification
Wrong document type uploaded - system offers 'Verify manually' option
Name/sponsor mismatch detected - system offers 'Ignore and continue' option with delay warning
Unreadable document - prompt re-upload or manual verification
Syrian passport with known MRZ issues - passport number in unusual location, system handles gracefully
Must complete submission by clicking 'Continue Submission' to finalize
Users can save drafts to complete later
Conditional documents may become required based on dependent characteristics
Multiple dependents - each requires separate endorsement submission
Dependents must be removed along with the principal employee
Offboarding means complete removal of employee access
Users can save drafts of endorsement requests to complete later
Employee with multiple dependents - all dependents automatically removed
Syrian passport with known MRZ issues - system handles gracefully
Error messages explain that not correcting will cause delays

what_to_do.task: Assign already-insured member to existing health insurance policy
steps: Navigate to Employee Profile
Click on Health Insurance section
Click on 'Marked as already insured'
Select the relevant insurance plan from available options
Review plan details
Click 'Continue' to assign the policy
what_to_do.expected_outcome: Member marked as already insured is now assigned to the selected health insurance policy on the platform. Policy assignment is recorded and member appears in policy roster.
rules_and_constraints: The member must already be insured externally but not yet assigned to a specific policy on the platform
Member must have 'Marked as already insured' status in system
Only available insurance plans can be selected
Assignment is immediate upon clicking 'Continue'
edge_cases: Member has multiple insurance policies - user must select the correct one
Plan options may be limited based on company's active policies

source_articles.title: How to assign new members to an existing Health Insurance Policy on the platform

what_to_do.task: Track employee addition request status online
steps: Navigate to Endorsements section
Click on 'Additions' tab
Locate the specific addition request
View request status and current stage
Check submission date and effective date
Hover over status to view progress details
If effective date changes, review reason (indicated by info icon)
If turnaround time exceeded, contact support at endorsements@bayzat.com
Download Certificate of Insurance (COI) if available
what_to_do.expected_outcome: User can view real-time status of employee addition request from submission through completion. User understands current processing stage, expected effective date, and any required actions. COI can be downloaded within 30 days of completion.
rules_and_constraints: Requests go through specific stages: Under Review, With Insurer, Attention Required, Addition Completed
COI can be downloaded within 30 days of completion
Users must email endorsements@bayzat.com if turnaround time is exceeded
Must provide employee name and company name when contacting support
Effective dates may change during processing
If 'Attention Required' status appears, additional documents may be needed
Requests go through 4 specific stages: Under Review, With Insurer, Attention Required, Removal Completed
Users must provide complete documentation
Effective dates may change with notification
If turnaround time exceeded, users should email endorsements@bayzat.com
Must provide employee and company name when contacting support
edge_cases: Potential need for additional documents - 'Attention Required' status triggers notification
Effective date may change - indicated by info icon with explanation
Requirement to check and follow up on request status if TAT exceeded
COI download window closes after 30 days
If turnaround time is exceeded, users should email endorsements@bayzat.com
Changes in effective date will trigger an info icon
Missing documents may halt the removal process
'Attention Required' status indicates additional documents needed

source_articles.title: How to track the status of your addition request online
How to track the status of your removal request online

steps: Navigate to Employee Profile
Go to Dependents section
Select Insurance tab
Locate dependent to remove
Click remove or delete option
Add removal details (reason, effective date, etc.)
Add optional comments or special requirements
Choose action: Save as Draft OR Submit Removal Request
Track removal status in Endorsements section
what_to_do.expected_outcome: Dependent is removed from health insurance policy. Removal request is submitted and tracked. User receives confirmation and can monitor processing status.
rules_and_constraints: Users must provide details when removing a dependent
Can save drafts of requests to complete later
Should be aware of expected turnaround times (TATs)
Removal reason must be specified
edge_cases: Users should check TATs and follow up if necessary
Can save drafts to complete later
Multiple dependents - each requires separate removal request

gaps_identified: Finance Manager: No tasks identified. Endorsements feature does not directly involve finance operations (payroll, settlements, deductions). Finance Manager may need visibility into payment tracking for endorsements (Phase 2 PRD mentions 'Awaiting Client Payment' status), but this is not a primary workflow for this persona.
No tickets provided for analysis. Unable to identify limitations or issues affecting any persona.

execution_order: 2. EXPLORATION - Navigate to feature, discover all UI elements using exploration_phase instructions
3. IDENTIFY ENTITY - Determine the primary item/entity using primary_entity discovery
4. CRUD TEST - Test create, read, update, delete for the primary entity
5. WORKFLOW CHECK - Verify if feature has workflow triggers/actions
6. WHAT_TO_DO - Execute each task from what_to_do array, validate expected outcomes
7. DOCUMENTATION - Capture screenshots of every significant state change
8. REPORT - Generate validation report with pass/fail for each task and issue
